task: work on the drift-gated wiki (generate, heal, extend, or debug hunch wiki)
steps: Read docs/wiki.md first — the five patterns (derived view, home pairing, freshness state machine, jurisdiction grading, adoption) with pins to their governing decisions

Orient in code: src/wiki/wiki.ts (homes, packs, wikiStatus state machine, generateWiki, computeWikiDrift), src/wiki/adopt.ts (healed copies), src/core/docscan.ts (doc grading), `wiki` command in src/cli/index.ts

Before editing, run hunch_why on the file and hunch_check_constraints — wiki decisions chain under topics wiki.derived-view / wiki.specs-ledger / wiki.doc-grading-jurisdiction / wiki.freshness-closure

Any new rendered output MUST enter the freshness closure: hash its inputs, register a manifest entry (reserved ids: _index, _specs, doc:<rel>), route through pageState() — never write a page the manifest doesn't track
Any new read MUST respect the home's source gate (home.source: public → store.json.loadAll, all → store.recs) — overlay data may only ever land in the overlay repo
Test with test/wiki.test.ts fixtures (tempStore/overlayStore); every leak-sensitive change needs a LEAK CHECK test asserting committed surfaces carry no overlay text/ids
Verify live: npm run typecheck && npx tsx --test test/wiki.test.ts, then bash demo/wiki.sh (full loop: ground → supersede → drift → heal/adopt → release)

On this repo: NEVER commit a public wiki (gitignored + blocking constraint); regenerate the private one with `hunch wiki --heal --private` after graph changes, and expect every hunch_record_decision to stale pages — heal after capturing
gotchas: Pin ids in hunch:topic markers must be alphanumeric after dec_ (the marker regex rejects underscores — real hex ids are fine)
Adopted-copy healing rewrites pins ONLY inside their marker; bare decision ids in prose are never rewritten
The freshness hash covers inputs, never LLM prose or page bytes — bytes have their own tamper field in the manifest
wikiStatus is the single slug authority (component slugs, adopted slugs, reserved readme/specs) — renderers receive paths, they never re-derive them
generateWiki --heal with an empty component graph is legal (orphan cleanup); a plain generate is not
Relation links between pages are NOT hash-covered (accepted residual in dec_c205c26472) — a slug reassignment can leave a fresh page linking to a renamed sibling
outcome: typecheck green, test/wiki.test.ts green, demo/wiki.sh completes the full ground→supersede→adopt→release loop, hunch drift clean, and no wiki artifact committed to the public repo
